questions.question: The phrase TREND may be found in:
The visibility measurement in TAFS / METARS is supplied in:
To measure vertical distance above MSL the altimeter subscale should be set:
What is meant by the term dewpoint?

options.a: the temperature at which condensation and evaporation are equal
options.b: the temperature at which dew will always form
options.c: the temperature to which air must be cooled to become saturated
options.d: the spread between actual temperature and the temperature during evaporation

questions.question: The suffix nimbus used in naming clouds means:

options.a: a cloud with extensive vertical development
options.c: a middle cloud containing ice pellets

questions.question: Moist, stable air flowing upslope can be expected to:
What are the characteristics of unstable air?

options.c: nimbostratus clouds and good surface visibility
options.d: nimbostratus clouds and poor surface visibility

questions.question: A moist unstable air mass is characterised by:
In which environment is aircraft structural ice most likely to have the highest accumulation rate?
Why is frost considered hazardous to flight?

options.a: the increased weight requires a shorter take off distance
options.b: frost has no effect on basic aerodynamic shape of the airfoil
options.d: frost causes early airflow separation resulting in a loss of lift

questions.question: Crests of standing mountain waves may be marked by stationary, lens shaped clouds known as:
Hazardous windshear is commonly encountered near the ground:

options.a: near thunderstorms and during periods when the wind velocity is stronger than 35 kts
options.b: during periods when the wind velocity is stronger than 35 kts and near mountain valleys
options.c: during periods of strong temperature inversion and near thunderstorms
options.d: near mountain valleys and on the windward side of a hill or mountain

questions.question: Which conditions result in the formation of frost?

options.b: the collecting surface's temperature is at or below freezing and small droplets of moisture fall on the collecting surface
options.c: the temperature of the collecting surface is at or below the dewpoint of the adjacent air and the dewpoint is below freezing
options.d: small drops of moisture falling on the collecting surface when the surrounding air temperature is at or below freezing

questions.question: Possible mountain wave turbulence would be anticipated when winds of:

options.a: 20 Kts or greater blow across a mountain ridge, and the air is unstable
options.b: 40 Kts or greater blow across a mountain ridge, and the air is stable
options.c: 30 Kts or greater blow down a mountain valley, and the air is unstable
options.d: 50 Kts or greater blow parallel to a mountain peak, and the air is stable

questions.question: Frost which has not been removed from the lifting surfaces of an airplane before flight:

options.a: may prevent the airplane from becoming airborne
options.b: will change the camber (curvature of the wing) thereby increasing lift during the take off
options.c: may cause the airplane to become airborne with a lower angle of attack and at a lower indicated airspeed
options.d: would present no problems since frost will blow off when the airplane starts moving during take off

questions.question: A pilot can expect a wind shear zone in a temperature inversion, whenever the wind speed at 2000 to 4000 feet above the surface is at least:
What feature is normally associated with the cumulus stage of a thunderstorm?

options.d: beginning of rain at the surface

questions.question: Which weather phenomenon signals the beginning of the mature stage of a thunderstorm?

options.a: the appearance of an anvil top
options.c: growth rate of cloud is maximum

questions.question: A non-frontal, narrow band of active thunderstorms that often develop ahead of a cold front is known as:
Thunderstorms which generally produce the most intense hazard to aircraft are:
Upon encountering severe turbulence, which condition should the pilot attempt to maintain?
What conditions are necessary for the formation of thunderstorms?

options.a: lifting force, high humidity and unstable conditions
options.b: high humidity, high temperature and cumulus clouds
options.c: low pressure, high humidity and cumulus clouds
options.d: lifting force, high temperature and unstable conditions

questions.question: During the life cycle of a thunderstorm, which stage is characterised predominately by downdrafts?
Low level turbulence can occur and icing can become hazardous in:
Thunderstorms reach their greatest intensity during the:
In which situation is advection fog most likely to form?

options.a: a warm moist air mass on the windward side of mountains
options.b: an air mass moving inland from the coast in winter
options.c: a light breeze blowing colder air out to sea
options.d: warm moist air settling over a warmer surface under no wind conditions

questions.question: Which weather phenomenon is always associated with a thunderstorm?
Which is considered to be the most hazardous condition when flying in the vicinity of thunderstorms?
If there is thunderstorm activity in the vicinity of an airport at which you plan to land, which hazardous and invincible atmospheric phenomenon might be expected on the landing approach?
The development of thermals depends upon:
The layer of the Earth's atmosphere which most concerns us as aviators is called the:
The international standard atmosphere is defined for MSL as:

options.a: 1015.25 mbs, 15°C and density of 1225 grams per cubic metre
options.b: 1015.25 mbs, 15°C with a lapse rate of 1.98°C per 1000 feet
options.c: 1013.25 mbs, 15°C with a lapse rate of 1.98°C per 1000 feet and a humidity of 5%
options.d: 1013.25 mbs, 15°C a temperature lapse rate of 1.98°C per 1000 feet and a density of 1225 grams per cubic metre

questions.question: The most active (mature stage) of a thunderstorm can be recognised by:

options.a: rain commencing to fall from the bottom of the cloud
options.b: a general reduction of turbulence in the lower layers
options.c: the formation of the characteristic anvil shaped projection at the top of the cloud
options.d: an increase in cloud's rate of vertical development

questions.question: Your weather forecast states the surface wind is SW backing SE. In compass degrees this is:
A weather report is a statement of the weather conditions:

options.c: That are being experienced at present
options.d: That are forecast for the following day

questions.question: You notice large cumulus clouds building up. Which of the following types of precipitation would you expect?
The amount of water vapor in the air as a percentage of what the air could hold at the same temperature and pressure is known as:
You are flying in a cloud at an ambient temperature of 5°C. Which of the following is most probable?
In an anticyclone the winds blow:

options.a: Clockwise round the high pressure in the Northern hemisphere
options.b: Clockwise round the high pressure in the Southern hemisphere
options.c: Clockwise round the low pressure in the Northern hemisphere

questions.question: You are flying towards a range of hills and have strong tail wind. Where would you expect to find upcurrents?

options.c: On both sides of the hills
options.d: You will not encounter any up currents
